pr.title: Decompile 9 fighter, library, game, and menu functions
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: PR #2344 replaced several placeholder decomp stubs with C implementations across fighter, game, library, menu, and player-stat code, and updated related headers/prototypes and one THP struct layout. Review evidence centered on scoping a `#pragma dont_inline` change in `mnname.c` with `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop` so the pragma did not affect the whole file. Evidence is mixed because the PR body claims 9 functions and 100% match by overnight script, while the loaded changed files omit `toy.c`/`un_80307828` and the bot report shows 4 new matches plus 4 near-match improvements.

key_files.role: Decompiled `fn_800DA1D8`, initializing CatchWait grab setup, callbacks, captured joint storage, grab effect spawn, and victim handling.
Updated `fn_800DA1D8` prototype from `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` to `void fn_800DA1D8(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`.
Added `mp/mplib.h` include and decompiled `ftCh_Damage2_Anim` for Crazy Hand damage recovery behavior.
Decompiled `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_0_Anim`, including aerial throw completion, Nana availability check, and partner follow-up path.
Decompiled `ftCo_800C0134` with scoped pragmas, collision animation reset, character callback, and table-driven `lb_800144C8` calls.
Changed `fn_8016F160` from void wrapper to returning the result of `pl_80039418`.
Updated `fn_8016FFD4` prototype from unknown to `void fn_8016FFD4(struct lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t*, int, int)`.
Decompiled `fn_8017F1B8`, iterating 256 game event slots, checking masks, dispatching handlers, and calling `fn_8016FFD4`.
Decompiled `fn_8001F06C`, an interrupt-protected THP decoder state/frame counter update routine.
Expanded `THPDecComp` fields at offsets 0x68, 0x6C, 0x108, and 0x10C and updated `fn_8001F06C` prototype.
Decompiled `mnName_80238A04`, including a loop over name-entry JObjs and scoped `#pragma dont_inline`; this file received review feedback about pragma scoping.
Updated `mnName_80238A04` prototype to `void mnName_80238A04(HSD_GObj* gobj, u8 target, u8 flag)`.
Decompiled `pl_80037DF4`, updating player attack counters from event flags.

what_changed: Replaced decomp placeholders with C bodies for `fn_800DA1D8`, `ftCh_Damage2_Anim`, `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_0_Anim`, `ftCo_800C0134`, `fn_8017F1B8`, `fn_8001F06C`, `mnName_80238A04`, and `pl_80037DF4` in the loaded diff.
Updated headers to replace several `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations with typed prototypes for newly decompiled or newly understood functions.
Adjusted `THPDecComp` in `lbmthp.h` to expose fields used by `fn_8001F06C`, including signed fields at 0x68, 0x6C, 0x108, and 0x10C.
Changed `fn_8016F160` to return the result of `pl_80039418`, supporting its use as a nonzero condition in `fn_8017F1B8`.
Scoped `#pragma dont_inline` in `mnname.c` with `#pragma push` and `#pragma pop` after review noted the unscoped pragma affected all following functions.

smart_moves.text: Updated owning headers alongside recovered bodies, reducing unknown prototypes rather than keeping local or stale declarations.
Recovered struct fields in `THPDecComp` where the decompiled THP routine needs typed accesses, avoiding opaque padding for actively used offsets.
Used a natural `for (i = 0; i < 256; i++)` loop for game event slot dispatch and `for (i = 0x18; i < 0x1B; i++)` for repeated menu JObj animation updates.
Accepted review feedback by scoping a function-specific pragma with push/pop so the compiler setting did not leak to the rest of the translation unit.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered body proves a function signature, update the owning header from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to the real return and argument types in the same change.
If a matching function requires a pragma, scope it tightly around the function with `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop`; otherwise it can unintentionally affect later functions in the file.
Expose real struct fields when a decompiled routine repeatedly uses those offsets, as shown by `THPDecComp` fields added for `fn_8001F06C`.
Use ordinary source loops for repeated indexed behavior when the diff supports it, such as event slot iteration and repeated menu JObj animation calls.

naming_conventions.convention: The PR retained existing address-style names such as `fn_800DA1D8`, `fn_8017F1B8`, `fn_8001F06C`, and `mnName_80238A04` rather than inventing new semantic names.
Short local role names were used where roles are clear, including `fp` for `Fighter*`, `gobj` for game object parameters, `jobj` for `HSD_JObj*`, and `attrs`/`da` for attribute pointers.
Unknown struct or move-state fields remained conservatively named, such as `unk_108`, `unk_10C`, `mv.ch.unk0.xC`, and `mv.pp.unk_80123954.x0`, where the PR evidence did not prove richer names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The diff shows the tactic, but no inline explanation or narrow objdiff evidence beyond the bot/PR reports is loaded.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Function-scoped pragmas were used for matching-sensitive functions: `ftCo_800C0134` uses `auto_inline off` and `global_optimizer off`; `mnName_80238A04` uses `dont_inline on` wrapped in push/pop.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Review explicitly confirmed pragma leakage risk for `mnname.c`; no separate review comment explains the `ftcolanim.c` pragma need.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: `fn_8001F06C` uses a labeled `goto end` to share interrupt restoration before returning.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The shape is present in the recovered C; the PR evidence does not explain alternatives tried.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Explicit casts such as `(u8)` and `(u32)` appear in `fn_8017F1B8` and `mnName_80238A04`, likely preserving comparison/mask code shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The exact assembly reason is not documented in comments.

review_feedback.feedback: `#pragma dont_inline on` lacked a `#pragma push` before it and `#pragma pop` after the function, so it incorrectly affected all following functions in the file.
review_feedback.resolution: Author replied that `#pragma push` / `#pragma pop` wrapping was added; the loaded diff shows `mnName_80238A04` wrapped by push/pop.

follow_up_queries: PR2344 toy.c un_80307828 changed file missing from loaded diff
mnName_80238A04 pragma dont_inline push pop review
ftCo_800C0134 global_optimizer off auto_inline off rationale
decomp PR pragma leakage affects following functions

notes: Changed-file metadata and raw diff are complete for 15 loaded files and support the file-level changes summarized here.

PR body claims 9 functions and includes `un_80307828` in `toy.c`, but loaded changed files and raw diff do not include `toy.c`; this intake does not treat the trophy viewer function as evidenced by the diff.

PR body says all 9 functions 100% match by overnight script, while the bot report in loaded text corpus shows 4 new 100% matches and 4 improvements below 100%; verification claims are therefore recorded cautiously.
Review evidence is strong for the `mnname.c` pragma scoping issue and fix.
Some matching tactics such as `PAD_STACK`, `global_optimizer off`, and casts are visible in diff but not justified by detailed objdiff notes in the loaded PR evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Scope matching pragmas with push/pop
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2344 review shows that a `#pragma dont_inline on` added for one menu function needed `#pragma push` before it and `#pragma pop` after it; otherwise the pragma affected the rest of the file.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover headers with recovered bodies
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2344 updated headers from unknown signatures to typed prototypes for functions whose bodies were recovered, including `fn_800DA1D8`, `fn_8017F1B8`, `fn_8001F06C`, and `mnName_80238A04`.

source_update_candidates.title: Emphasize push/pop scoping for single-function pragmas
source_update_candidates.text: When using `#pragma dont_inline`, `#pragma auto_inline off`, `#pragma global_optimizer off`, or similar matching pragmas for one function, wrap the affected region with `#pragma push` and `#pragma pop`; unscoped pragmas can silently affect later functions in the same file.
source_update_candidates.reason: The existing standards already discourage broad pragma use, but this PR provides concise review evidence for the specific push/pop failure mode.

source_update_candidates.title: Flag PR body versus bot-report verification mismatches during intake
source_update_candidates.text: For PR postmortems, distinguish author-stated verification from bot-reported match percentages when they conflict. In PR #2344, the body claims all 9 functions 100% match, while the loaded bot report lists 4 new 100% matches and 4 near-match improvements below 100%, and the loaded diff omits the claimed `toy.c` function.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful for curator workflow; not a decomp standard unless promoted separately.

rejection_notes: Do not promote `un_80307828` or `toy.c` as a changed-file fact from this slice because it appears only in the PR body and not in the loaded changed files or raw diff.
Do not promote the PR body's 'All 9 functions 100% match' as an accepted validation result without reconciling it with the bot report showing partial improvements.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the explicit pragma scoping comment in `mnname.c`.
Do not turn visible `PAD_STACK` or pragma use into general style guidance without stronger verifier evidence.
